programs.title: King Charles III
programs.desc: After the death of Elizabeth II, her son Charles (Tim Pigott-Smith) takes over power in Great Britain.During his first meeting with Prime Minister Tristan Evans (Adam James), he learns about the draft law regulating the rights of the press, which has already been adopted by both the House of Commons and the House of Lords and is only awaiting the king's consent.Charles refuses to sign it, fearing that the new law will limit press freedom and allow the government to use censorship.Then, due to the prime minister's threat to introduce the law without royal consent, he dissolves parliament.Soon a wave of protests begins to sweep across the country, with the most violent taking place in London.The difficult situation at Buckingham Palace is not improved by a sex scandal involving the ruler's younger son, Prince Henry (Richard Goulding), related to the publication of compromising photos of him with his lover, Jessica (Tamara Lawrance).Charles's older son, William (Oliver Chris), and his wife Catherine (Charlotte Riley) also do not support the king.The young man accuses his father of causing the crisis and demands that he abdicate.A film version of Mike Bartlett's play of the same title.The author is also the author of the script for this production.

programs.title: The Knick
programs.desc: New York, the first years of the 20th century.Brilliant surgeon Dr. John Thackery (Clive Owen) leads a brave team of young medics at Knickerbocker Hospital.The doctor introduces pioneering medical solutions while struggling with cocaine addiction.His team is making breakthroughs in medicine at a time when antibiotics do not yet exist and patient mortality is terrifyingly high.The group of distinguished medical staff includes, among others:protégé Everett Gallinger (Eric Johnson) and Algernon Edwards (Andre Holland), a promising surgeon from Europe who was included in the team against Thackery's wishes.The series is directed by Oscar winner Steven Soderbergh.

actor: Dr. John W. Thackery - Clive Owen

programs.title: Last Week Tonight With John Oliver 13
programs.desc: Another season of a show full of irony and sarcasm, hosted by John Oliver.In each episode, the host summarizes the events from the world of politics from the last week.

programs.title: The Pitt
programs.desc: Staff members are following up on information about a stolen ambulance.They also place bets.Michael removes the endotracheal tube from Mr. Spencer.He also tries to comfort his children.Nurse Mateo Diaz is insulted by racist Doug Driscoll, who is upset about the long time he has spent in the waiting room.Javadi sews up a patient's wound and corrects an error in her medical records.Mel and Mohan treat a baby with hair wrapped around a part of its body, creating a kind of tourniquet.Dana and the other nurses tend to Mr. Krakozhia, who is waiting for a bed in the psychiatric ward.They have trouble giving him the injection.

programs.title: Euphoria
programs.desc: The further fate of a group of friends who left school behind and entered adulthood.The action moves forward several years.Rue (Zendaya Coleman) is no longer a lost teenager, but a person involved in the criminal world.He goes to interrogation in connection with smuggling drugs from Mexico.Cassie (Sydney Sweeney) and Maddy's (Alexa Demie) relationship has improved, but old wounds haven't healed at all.The first woman's relationship with Nate (Jacob Elordi) develops, but not towards a happy ending.The remaining characters confront their own choices, addiction and violence.They are looking for redemption and answers to the question about their place in the world.
